posts.content: I am forever saying that if refusal isn't a live option in any decision making process about "AI", then no ethical practice is possible.  You've got to be able to stop if the thing is unacceptable.
Join us tomorrow!
Wait, how did this get into my feed without @hypervisible.blacksky.app already quoting it
If your ostensibly critical paper talks about "recent advances in AI" I have a hard time taking it seriously. Advances towards what? Measured how?
Today!

Have you been asked by a medical provider recently for consent to have an "AI" scribe record your visit? Us, too. And we have **thoughts**

buttondown.com/maiht3k/arch...

NYC & area folks -- @alexhanna.bsky.social and I are doing our first ever LIVE TAPING of Mystery AI Hype Theater 3000 at Starr Bar in Brooklyn on Thursday April 30th

Think of just how cool that crowd is going to be and you can be a part of it too!

www.eventbrite.com/e/mystery-ai...
Favorite new to me word, from a review of The AI Con: demythifying.
The plagiarism machine doesn't just traffic in text.
Reading Anthropic's recent nonsense about "emotion vectors" and was struck by this remark. Is there really such a taboo? Because we see anthropomorphizing language about "AI" systems *all the time*.
This is written by and for linguists, but I suspect there is useful information here no matter what your field, if your field touches on people.

Really proud to have been a part of this paper, with Rob, Martin, Alicia, Alex, Anna and @kirbyconrod.bsky.social 

Check it out for how to conceptualize "race" and "ethnicity" in linguistic research. Spoiler alert: it's not something that can be addressed with word choice at the very end.

Usually, when I get interviewed for a piece on something like "AI consciousness" I am relegated to the skeptics box --- some short paragraph near the end. So it is a nice change to see this piece by @hollybaxter.bsky.social 

Short 🧵>>
www.the-independent.com/tech/ai-news...

@alexhanna.bsky.social and I are so excited to announce that THE AI CON has been selected as Book in Common for 2026-27 at Cal State Chico!

We're excited for thousands of folks to read and engage with our work. We'll be visiting campus on April 7, 2027 for a public event.

Seattle friends! This event on May 17, with Shelley Fairweather-Vega at Folio: The Seattle Athenaeum should be really fun. Join us!

Tip for dealing with busy people: If you're asking someone to speak (esp. for free) at some event, AND you expect them to spend some time on a meeting beforehand to plan how it will go, put this in the initial invitation. 

Demanding extra time after someone has already agreed is rude, honestly.

“‘AI’ might not be good for xyz, but you can’t deny that it’s helpful for programming” -- sound familiar? On the next Mystery AI Hype Theater 3000 @alexhanna.bsky.social and I will be digging into that bullshit. Join us for the livestream: 

Monday, May 11, noon PT

I guess I'm glad this is out there, but also I am infuriated that people have to spend their time doing this. OF COURSE synthetic text extruding machines are going to output something that *looks like* but is not an analysis.

You can step off that bandwagon. Life is better over here!

So "don't feed the trolls" usually means "don't feed the trolls content to keep them going" but sometimes I think it maybe also means "don't feed the trolls to one's followers" (by e.g. boosting a particularly bad take for others to see).

xPosts.worthReading: It highlights the importance of accountability in AI development by rejecting deterministic narratives.

interestSummary: Emily M. Bender is focused on critiquing the hype and unethical practices surrounding AI, particularly through her work on the Mystery AI Hype Theater 3000 podcast and her advocacy for the necessity of refusal in AI decision-making. She is also engaged in linguistic research on race and ethnicity, and in public education about the dangers of anthropomorphizing language models.
